codeReview.summary: The fork-and-rebrand feature successfully cloned the ClawHip repo, rebranded the binary and crate name to 'hermip', removing OpenClaw/Codex/Claude Code plugins (plugins/codex/, plugins/claude-code/, skills/omc/, skills/omx/, integrations/omx/), and set up .factory/ infrastructure. The binary builds and 409 tests pass. However, there are significant remnants of the ClawHip rebrand that were not completed: (1) Multiple source files still reference CLAWHIP_ environment variables as primary names, (2) extensive 'clawhip' references remain in README.md, SKILL.md, CI workflows, scripts, and documentation files, (3) Codex and Claude Code provider code remains in src/hooks/, src/plugins.rs, and src/cli.rs, (4) SKILL.md and README.md still describe ClawHip rather than Hermip. The .factory/ infrastructure (init.sh, services.yaml) is properly set up.

issues.description: CLAWHIP_ env vars are still primary: CLAWHIP_DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, CLAWHIP_DAEMON_URL, CLAWHIP_GITHUB_TOKEN, CLAWHIP_DISCORD_API_BASE, CLAWHIP_TMUX_BIN, CLAWHIP_GIT_BIN, CLAWHIP_PLUGIN_DIR, CLAWHIP_SKIP_STAR_PROMPT all use the CLAWHIP_ prefix as primary names. AGENTS.md states 'No references to clawhip in source code (compat layer comments are OK)'. These are not compat layer comments — they are primary env var names used in production code paths.

Codex and ClaudeCode hook providers (HookProvider::Codex, HookProvider::ClaudeCode) with install/uninstall code remain in the codebase. The feature description explicitly says to remove 'hook code for Codex/Claude'. While the Hermes provider was added alongside, the legacy providers were not removed.

CLI subcommand 'deliver' references Codex/Claude session wrappers: 'Deliver a prompt into an existing hooked tmux-backed Codex/Claude session (including OMC/OMX wrappers).' The 'hooks' subcommand description says 'Install and manage provider-native hook forwarding for Codex and Claude Code' without mentioning Hermes. The HookProvider enum still includes Codex and ClaudeCode.

README.md still contains extensive 'clawhip' references (140+ occurrences). It still describes ClawHip rather than Hermip, references OpenClaw/Codex workflows, and mentions the old binary name 'clawhip' throughout. This should be updated to reflect the Hermip rebrand.
SKILL.md still references 'clawhip' in name, description, and all command examples. It needs full rebranding to Hermip.
CI workflow still references 'clawhip-linux-x86_64' and './target/release/clawhip'. Should be updated to hermip.
CLAWHIP_DIR constant is '.hermip' but CLAWHIP_PROJECT_FILE is '.hermip/project.json'. While the directory name is correct, the Rust constant names still say CLAWHIP.
Script references CLAWHIP_REPO and uses 'clawhip' in examples. Should be updated.
Error messages reference 'OMX bridge', 'OMC/OMX-compatible', and Codex/Claude names. While compat layer references are OK per AGENTS.md, user-facing error messages should use Hermip branding.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CLAWHIP_ env var naming convention is inconsistent. Some new env vars use HERMIP_ prefix (HERMIP_CONFIG, HERMIP_DAEMON_PORT, HERMIP_DAEMON_BASE_URL) but many code paths still use CLAWHIP_ as the primary name. A clear convention should be documented: HERMIP_ as primary, CLAWHIP_ as backward-compat fallback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/config.rs has both HERMIP_* overrides and CLAWHIP_* primary vars; src/discord.rs, src/source/git.rs, src/source/tmux.rs, src/lifecycle.rs all use CLAWHIP_*

sharedStateObservations.observation: The plugins/codex/ and plugins/claude-code/ directories were removed, but the hook provider enum (HookProvider::Codex, HookProvider::ClaudeCode) and related install/uninstall logic remain in src/hooks/mod.rs and src/cli.rs. The rebrand was partial — the Hermes provider was added but legacy providers were not removed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/hooks/mod.rs lines 67-88 still handle HookProvider::Codex and HookProvider::ClaudeCode; src/cli.rs line 69 mentions 'Codex/Claude session'; src/plugins.rs lines 181 and 197 still define Codex and Claude Code bridge plugins

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/services.yaml correctly defines the hermip daemon start/stop/healthcheck commands on port 25294. .factory/init.sh correctly builds the release binary. Both are properly set up.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml has hermip service with start: 'hermip daemon start --config hermip.toml', stop: 'hermip daemon stop', healthcheck: 'curl -sf http://localhost:25294/health', port: 25294

summary: The fork-and-rebrand feature successfully cloned the ClawHip repo, renamed the binary to 'hermip', removed plugin directories (codex, claude-code, omc, omx), and set up .factory/ infrastructure. The binary builds and all 409 tests pass. However, the rebrand is incomplete with blocking issues: (1) CLAWHIP_ env vars remain as primary names in source code (not compat layer), (2) Codex/ClaudeCode hook providers and related code were not removed from src/hooks/, src/cli.rs, src/plugins.rs, (3) CLI help text still references Codex/Claude/OMC/OMX. Non-blocking: README.md and SKILL.md still have extensive clawhip references, CI workflows still reference the old binary name, and several source files use CLAWHIP_ constant names.
